lesson.menu_title: Bigger, Better, Different — Comparatives in Global HR
lesson.menu_desc: Comparing HR data and teams with comparative adjectives (short, long, irregular, and equality) -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 15 -- Bigger, Better, Different -- Comparatives
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Murielle Xavier | Aula 15 -- Comparatives

listenings.text: Hello team. Here is a quick comparison of our two sites. The head office is larger than the branch, but its turnover is considerably higher. The branch is smaller, yet it performs better on almost every metric. The gap in engagement is significant. The branch is also more cost-effective, because its overtime is lower. The difference in results is much wider than the difference in size. In short, the smaller site is more competitive than the bigger one. I think the branch is the stronger model, and we should learn from it.

Good morning. Let me compare this year with last year. Our turnover is slightly lower, which is good news. The disparity between departments, however, is bigger than before. Sales has a much higher attrition ratio than Finance. The margin between our best and worst teams is wider this year. There is also a clear discrepancy between our targets and our results. Still, overall we are more efficient than last year, and our costs are more comparable across regions. I am confident that next year will be even stronger.
